arts.ttl: Kiernan Dewsbury-Hall sends classy message to Leicester City star after injury blow
arts.sum: Kiernan Dewsbury-Hall has sent a classy message of support to former teammate and Leicester City star Abdul Fatawu following news of his season-ending injury. Dewsbury-Hall followed the Italian to west London after playing a huge part in helping Leicester return to the Premier League.

arts.ttl: Chelsea's international break hangover: Worrying stat emerges ahead of Leicester clash
arts.sum: A worrying stat emerges ahead of Chelsea's trip to the King Power Stadium for Saturday's lunchtime kick-off against Enzo Maresca's former club Leicester City.

arts.ttl: Reece James' harsh Chelsea truth explained as Enzo Maresca confirms latest injury setback - Bobby Vincent - football.london
arts.sum: Gusto's performances since signing for Chelsea have meant James' absence has not really been felt in that sense. "He felt something and we don't want to take any risks for the weekend," the Chelsea head coach continued.

arts.ttl: Chelsea face anxious wait to see how long Reece James will be out for with captain to undergo scan on hamstring injury next week
arts.sum: The Blues captain is set to undergo a scan on a hamstring problem next week, as reported by The Telegraph.

arts.sum: The current Chelsea manager is certainly getting a tune out of a number of his first team players this season. Enzo Maresca has remained faithful...

arts.ttl: I didn't want to leave Chelsea for Leicester City but two transfers left me with no choice
arts.sum: Frank Sinclair is often a forgotten name at Chelsea even though he played a key role in their domestic and European success before their big-money takeover in 2003. Sinclair went on to become a mainstay throughout the next decade and played a key role in their 1997 FA Cup victory, the club's first major trophy in 27 years.

arts.ttl: Christopher Nkunku Chelsea surprise now imminent as Nicolas Jackson set to force latest decision - Tom Coley
arts.sum: It would be fair for Christopher Nkunku to think that his best chance of starting for Chelsea in the Premier League again this season is for Nicolas Jackson to max out the yellow card suspension meter. Nkunku is part of a select group of players who are genuinely fighting for Premier League minutes from those largely getting their game time in midweek (via the Conference League or previously the Carabao Cup, too).
